business.headline: Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Asian markets crash, will Dalal Street feel the aftershock on Monday?From South Korea's Kospi to Japan's Nikkei, Asian stock markets have tumbled sharply, leaving investors on edge. As Dalal Street prepares to reopen after a three-day holiday on Monday, will the BSE and NSE also open deep in the red?

From ITR filing to TDS: 3 major tax deadlines in July 2026 you shouldn't missWhether you're a salaried employee, a business owner or an employer, keeping track of these dates can help you avoid last-minute stress. Here are three key tax deadlines you should not miss in July 2026.

Will your family get your EPF pension after your death? Here's what you need to knowNo one likes to think about what happens after they're gone. But if you're an EPF member, knowing whether your family can continue receiving your pension could make a big difference to their financial future.

Will DA rise before the 8th Pay Commission? Here's what the latest data suggestsThe wait for the 8th Pay Commission continues, but all eyes are now on the next DA revision. As inflation remains elevated and the July review draws closer, is another DA hike just around the corner?

Govt restores commercial LPG supply to pre-West Asia crisis levelsThe move comes after the government reviewed the country's fuel availability and found that both domestic production and imported LPG supplies have improved.

AI reality check hits Asia as Seoul's Kospi crashes 8%, Tokyo's Nikkei tumbles 5%South Korea's KOSPI fell 8.2%, prompting authorities to halt program trading for 20 minutes after steep losses rippled across the market. Japan's Nikkei 225 dropped 5%, while Hong Kong's Hang Seng declined 2.4%.

What's the one big hurdle delaying India-US trade deal? Piyush Goyal explainsPiyush Goyal said India will bring the US trade deal into force only after Washington creates a tariff framework that favours Indian exporters over regional competitors. The position follows US legal changes that overturned reciprocal tariffs and forced both sides to revisit implementation.

Sebi may allow celebrity endorsements for financial brands. But here's a catchUnder the proposal, celebrities would be allowed to endorse a financial firm's brand or company name, subject to prescribed conditions, disclaimers and prior approvals.

Stock market holiday on Muharram: Are NSE and BSE open for trading on June 26?Planning to trade on Friday, June 26? With Muharram being observed across the country, many investors are wondering whether the NSE and BSE will remain open for trading?

Don't want to pay taxes online? Here's how to use the 'Pay at Bank Counter' optionNot comfortable paying taxes online? You're not alone. The Income Tax Department still allows taxpayers to pay their dues at authorised bank branches through the 'Pay at Bank Counter' facility.

India-US trade deal very close, but here's why it isn't signed yetPiyush Goyal said India and the US are close to finalising their trade deal. India wants tariff terms that give its exporters a clear edge over rivals.

Dalal Street pares gains: Sensex ends flat after climbing over 600 pointsEarly optimism driven by lower crude oil prices and a stronger rupee faded as investors booked profits, though benchmark indices still managed to end in positive territory.

Zerodha's Nithin Kamath has a warning for you against pyramid schemesAccording to figures cited by Kamath, more than 5.5 crore Indians have lost their savings to over 5,300 such schemes. The estimated losses stood at around Rs 10 lakh crore as of 2015, and he suggested that the actual figure could be much higher now.

Gold loses over Rs 51,000 from all-time high. Why is the yellow metal crashing?Gold has fallen sharply from its peak as silver also comes under selling pressure. The correction is being driven by rate-hike bets, a stronger dollar and shifting investor appetite.

United Spirits may cut 100 jobs as Diageo looks to trim costsUnited Spirits is set to lay off around 100 employees as Diageo pursues a global cost-cutting programme. The move comes amid a wider restructuring drive in India despite strong growth and continued investment in premium brands.

Amazon to invest $48 billion in India by 2030, CEO Andy Jassy meets PM ModiAccording to Jassy, the company aims to support 3.8 million jobs in India by 2030 and help generate $80 billion in cumulative e-commerce exports.

Sensex jumps over 600 points: Why are stock markets rallying today?Benchmark indices extended their recovery on Thursday as softer crude, firm Asian cues and gains in banks and autos lifted sentiment. The move has eased near-term macro concerns, though investors remain watchful of the monsoon and global central bank signals.

EPFO portal shutdown from June 26-28: How it will affect your EPF claimsPlanning to withdraw money from your EPF account or submit a claim this week? You may have to postpone it. EPFO has announced a three-day shutdown of key portal services as it upgrades its claims processing system.

GTA 5 made $10 billion. Here's what GTA 6 could earn if it repeats historyGrand Theft Auto V is the second best-selling video game ever made. As pre-orders open for its successor, here is what the numbers say about how big GTA 6 could really be.

entertainment.headline: Mohanlal makes Germany dance to his tunes, grooves to Dhanush's Megham Karukatha Mohanlal turned a star-studded event in Germany into a memorable celebration as he sang and danced to popular tracks, including Dhanush's Megham Karukatha, Illuminati and Kalapakkara. Videos of the Malayalam superstar's energetic stage performance with Saniya Iyappan and KS Chithra have gone viral online.

The trailer trap and forgetting art of surprise: How much is too much? What makes a great movie trailer? Audiences, editors, filmmakers and trade experts discuss the fine balance between building curiosity and giving away too much.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram sequel is on, confirms writer Raj Nidimoru Samantha Ruth Prabhu's Maa Inti Bangaaram is set to return with a sequel, writer Raj Nidimoru announced during the film's success meet in Vizag. The announcement comes after the Nandini Reddy directorial emerged as the highest-grossing women-led film and received widespread praise.

Veteran Tamil filmmaker, writer K Bhagyaraj dies of heart attack at 73 Veteran Tamil filmmaker, actor and screenwriter K Bhagyaraj died in Chennai at the age of 73 following a heart attack. His funeral is scheduled to be held at 1.30 pm at Chennai's Besant Nagar crematorium.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 8: Shahid film on track to surpass Teri Baaton Mein… Cocktail 2 has crossed Rs 114.67 crore at the worldwide box office after eight days, while its India nett collection has reached Rs 74.75 crore. Shahid Kapoor and Kriti Sanon's romantic drama is now closing in on the lifetime domestic earnings of Teri Baaton Mein Aisa Uljha Jiya.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 1: Akshay film scores big, beats Bhooth Bangla Akshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle has registered a decent opening at the box office, collecting Rs 29 crore worldwide on its first day despite mixed reviews. The comedy entertainer also surpassed the opening-day collections of the actor's recent films Bhooth Bangla and Jolly LLB 3.

Millie Bobby Brown-David Harbour reunite for Adolescence creator's spy series Actors Millie Bobby Brown and David Harbour will reunite in an untitled spy drama from Jack Thorne. The series casts them as estranged father and daughter at the centre of a mission crisis.

Akshay Kumar says Anil Kapoor-Nana Patekar are family, hints at Welcome 4 return Actor Akshay Kumar said actors Anil Kapoor and Nana Patekar remain part of the Welcome family despite missing Welcome To The Jungle. His remarks keep the door open for a reunion in a fourth film.

Rahul Ravindran defends Rashmika's The Girlfriend amid Pune murder case row Director Rahul Ravindran responded after social media users linked The Girlfriend to the Ketan Agarwal murder case. His post drew a distinction between individual crimes and the wider gender imbalance his film addresses.

Inside Khushbu Sundar's daughter's wedding with bridesmaids Trisha, Tamannaah Actor Tamannaah Bhatia shared glimpses from actor-politician Khushbu Sundar's daughter Avantika Sundar's star-studded wedding in Goa. The dreamy photos feature candid moments with Trisha Krishnan and Aarti Ravi.

Fast & Furious star Tyrese Gibson lands in Jamnagar as Ambani family guest Actor Tyrese Gibson arrived in Jamnagar after receiving a special invitation from the Ambani family. His airport appearance, marked by waves, smiles and a brief dance, drew local attention.

Actor Vishnu Vishal defends Rajinikanth, says how can he be jealous of CM Vijay? Actor Vishnu Vishal expressed his pain over superstar Rajinikanth having to publicly deny jealousy towards Tamil Nadu CM Vijay amidst online controversies. He highlighted the harsh scrutiny celebrities face on social media and urged fans not to confuse professional respect with personal rivalry.

Alliance Ep 1 review: Kushal-Zaid's history, Ravi Kishan's charm in a deja vu showRelease Date:Fri Jun 26, 2026 Prime Video's Alliance opens with 16 contestants entering Kunal Lok and forming early equations. Actor Kunal Kemmu stands out as host, but the premiere saves its only real spark for a late risk.

100 days of Dhurandhar 2: Rs 1,813 crore box office, all for Honsla Eendhan Badla Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge has completed 100 days in theatres, continuing its blockbuster run. The Aditya Dhar directorial has grossed Rs 1,813.38 crore worldwide, emerging as one of the biggest hits of recent years.

Half Man: Richard Gadd's show on toxic masculinity set for India streaming debut Actors Richard Gadd and Jamie Bell lead Half Man, a six-episode drama is set for its India streaming debut on July 3. The series traces a decades-long bond to examine masculinity, trauma, violence and emotional silence.

Mirzapur The Movie unveils Jitendra, Ravi Kishan, Abhishek Banerjee's intense looks The makers of Mirzapur: The Movie have unveiled new posters featuring Jitendra Kumar, Ravi Kishan and Abhishek Banerjee. The campaign broadens the film's character focus ahead of its 4 September 2026 theatrical release.
Why Kunal Kemmu took his first reality TV hosting gig with Alliance? Kunal Kemmu has stepped into reality television as the host of Prime Video's Alliance. The show premiered on June 26.

Prabhas recalls most stressful phase after Baahubali: I couldn't sleep In Baahubali: The Torchbearer, Prabhas said the film's success left him under intense pressure after release. He said that pressure shaped his choices and paved the way for Salaar and Kalki 2898 AD.

How many actors are in Welcome To The Jungle? Ahmed Khan reveals the count Director Ahmed Khan revealed the massive cast of Akshay Kumar-led comedy Welcome To The Jungle. He compared working with the crew to managing a daily music concert.

general news.headline: Trump to visit India early next year, his relationship with PM Modi is great: RubioMarco Rubio said President Trump is likely to visit India early next year amid ongoing bilateral trade talks between New Delhi and Washington.

Pahalgam mastermind, LeT terrorists attend funeral of Shoaib Akhtar's brotherA viral video shows Saifullah Kasuri, mastermind of the Pahalgam attack, and other Lashkar-e-Taiba figures at Shahid Akhtar's funeral in Islamabad. Shahid, the elder brother of Pakistani great Shoaib Akhtar, passed away earlier this week.

Iran's 'retreat and regret' warning after US bombs its missile and drone sitesThe Islamic Republic Guard Corps threatened Washington that the strikes would not go unanswered. Shortly after, it targeted several US military sites in the region.

Ketan Agarwal murder: Cricket brought Siya Goyal into Chetan Chaudhary's orbitAccording to police sources, Chetan, a cricket player, had played matches alongside Sahil, Siya's brother, and Siya would often accompany her brother during games.

India tops a global list nobody wants: 6 universities in paper retraction top 10India's rise in global research rankings is now shadowed by a sharp spike in paper retractions. With six Indian universities among the world's top 10 for retractions, concerns around plagiarism, fake peer reviews, and flawed incentives are growing, raising questions about credibility, funding, and the future of Indian science.

Jana Nayagan producer appointed Tamil Nadu Special Representative to DelhiTamil Nadu has appointed K Venkata Narayana as its Special Representative in New Delhi for one year. The move has drawn opposition criticism over his film industry background and proximity to C Joseph Vijay.

Veteran Tamil filmmaker, writer K Bhagyaraj dies of heart attack at 73Veteran Tamil filmmaker, actor and screenwriter K Bhagyaraj died in Chennai at the age of 73 following a heart attack. His funeral is scheduled to be held at 1.30 pm at Chennai's Besant Nagar crematorium.

Volkswagen faces first layoffs in 89-year history as China EV rivals force resetVolkswagen is considering one of the biggest restructurings in its history, with up to 1,00,000 jobs at risk and four German factories facing closure. The move comes as the auto giant struggles with falling market share in China, rising competition from Chinese EV makers, and growing pressure to cut costs.

We visited 10 Delhi neighbourhoods after SC's 'right to walk' ruling. Here's what we foundAfter the Supreme Court affirmed the right to walk, India Today Digital checked 10 Delhi locations. The result wasn't very surprising to be honest.

Hungary's Hannibal? Hospital worker accused of hoarding body parts, cannibalismA Hungarian hospital worker has been arrested after police allegedly found human remains in his Budapest apartment. Investigators claim he stole body parts from a hospital and abandoned cemeteries and admitted to eating some. Authorities are tracing the remains' origins and expanding the investigation.

IMD says monsoon is advancing. Then why isn't it raining in north India?The IMD says the southwest monsoon is advancing towards more northern states, even as rain remains scarce across much of North India. The gap reflects a northward monsoon circulation without the stronger low-pressure systems needed for widespread showers.

You're more sensitive than a clitoris: German MP's clapback at far-right lawmakerKathi Gebel, a 29-year-old lawmaker from the left-wing Die Linke party and the party's spokesperson on women's policy, was criticising motions proposed by the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) when she was interrupted by AfD MP Martin Reichardt.

How India can qualify for Women's T20 World Cup semi-finals: Scenarios explainedWomen's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have it all to play for against Australia in their final Group A clash, with a place in the semi-finals still up for grabs and the qualification race set to go down to the final day of the group stage.

Another 'did he fall asleep' moment for Trump? Religious panel meet sparks chatterDonald Trump appeared to briefly doze off during an Oval Office meeting with his Religious Liberty Commission. The moment drew social media attention and revived scrutiny over earlier episodes of apparent inattentiveness by the leader.
Engine trouble: Why European engine firms are making a beeline to IndiaA Franco-British contest is underway for India's future jet fighters.

PM Modi sets off for Seychelles visit; to meet world's oldest animalPM Modi will attend the Golden Jubilee celebrations of the country's National Day as the Chief Guest, marking 50 years of diplomatic relations between India and the Indian Ocean archipelago.

What is the cost of a life in India? Is it zero?Hundreds are killed in preventable tragedies every day in India. What value does India attach to the lives of its citizens? We go beyond the arguments here with facts.
Question Time: Weekly News QuizThink you read the headlines this week? Let's test that!

Live: No damage caused by US attack at Sirik port, say Iranian mediaUS-Iran Peace Deal Live Updates: Israel and Lebanon have signed a US-backed trilateral framework agreement in Washington after several rounds of negotiations, marking a significant diplomatic breakthrough aimed at reducing tensions along their shared border and advancing efforts toward a lasting ceasefire. The agreement comes even as Hezbollah has rejected the deal, warning it could deepen divisions within Lebanon. At the same time, fresh uncertainty has emerged after US President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating the ceasefire by launching a drone attack on a cargo ship near the Strait of Hormuz, an allegation Tehran denies while insisting it has the right to regulate the strategic waterway. Iran has also criticised the US and Gulf states over navigation in the strait, while the UAE has stepped up diplomatic engagement with Tehran, calling for freedom of navigation, regional stability and continued dialogue. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates on the Israel-Iran peace deal, the Israel-Lebanon framework agreement, Strait of Hormuz tensions, US diplomatic efforts, Hezbollah's response, shipping security and the wider geopolitical developments shaping the Middle East.

Will life insurance pay after a fire? Here's what every family should knowRecent fire tragedies have triggered questions over whether life insurance pays after accidental deaths. Experts say standard term plans generally cover such deaths unless false disclosures or policy exclusions apply.

Godzilla El Nino to hit sugarcane fields: Could it derail India's ethanol dreams?With a strong El Nino expected to impact India's monsoon, sugarcane yields are likely to drop. Here's why that could undermine India's efforts to adopt sustainable fuel alternatives and reduce dependency on oil imports.

Where is monsoon? Satellite captures rain clouds missing over IndiaSatellite imagery shows large parts of India without the deep rain clouds expected in late June despite the monsoon's advance. Forecasters say a developing system over the eastern Indian Ocean could revive rainfall, though its timing remains uncertain.

TVK minister faces storm over viral 'drug' video from IPL, he says it was medicineAfter an old video of Tamil Nadu Minister D Sarath Kumar handling a white powdery substance on a smartphone screen went viral, he clarified that it was medicine for his ailing child and not narcotics, as speculated online.

Welcome To The Jungle box office Day 1: Akshay film scores big, beats Bhooth BanglaAkshay Kumar's Welcome To The Jungle has registered a decent opening at the box office, collecting Rs 29 crore worldwide on its first day despite mixed reviews. The comedy entertainer also surpassed the opening-day collections of the actor's recent films Bhooth Bangla and Jolly LLB 3.

sports.headline: Samson's spot under threat? Sooryavanshi breathing down his neck, warns Manjrekar India tour of Ireland: Sanjay Manjrekar warns Sanju Samson to be careful as 15-year-old prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi piles on the pressure following India's historic and shocking T20I defeat against Ireland in the Belfast series opener.

Harshit Rana shrugs off painful past to deliver when India needed him most India vs Ireland, 1st T20I: Harshit Rana impressed on his return to the Indian side, claiming career-best T20I figures of 3 for 24, but his efforts went in vain as Ireland defeated India by 34 runs on Friday.

Belgium thrash NZ to reach knockouts, Iran face anxious wait after Egypt draw Belgium thrashed New Zealand 5-1 to finish top of Group G, while Egypt held Iran to a 1-1 draw in Seattle. The results sent Belgium and Egypt into the FIFA World Cup Round of 32, while Iran were left waiting to discover if they will qualify as one of the tournament's eight best third-placed teams.

Small islands, wild joy: Watch Cape Verde celebrate historic qualification in Houston FIFA World Cup 2026: Cape Verde reached the World Cup knockouts after drawing Saudi Arabia and waiting for Spain to beat Uruguay. The debutants became the smallest nation ever to do so and will now face Argentina in Round of 32.

Marcelo Bielsa shouts at reporter, accepts blame for Uruguay's World Cup exit FIFA World Cup: Uruguay manager Marcelo Bielsa shouted at a reporter during his post-match press conference after his side's group-stage exit on Friday before accepting full responsibility for the team's disappointing campaign.

Highlights: Belgium and Egypt go through, Iran face anxious wait FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights, Egypt vs Iran and Belgium vs New Zealand: A frenetic final matchday in Group G ends. Belgium are through to the Round of 32 as the group winners. They hammered New Zealand 5-1. Egypt and Iran played out a 1-1 draw in Seattle. Egypt are through and Iran need to wait until Sunday afternoon to learn their fate.

Cape Verde seal dream knockout berth, Uruguay crash out after Spain defeat FIFA World Cup: Uruguay's campaign came to a disappointing end on Friday after a 1-0 defeat to Spain, while tournament debutants Cape Verde completed a remarkable journey into the Round of 32 with a goalless draw against Saudi Arabia.

How India can qualify for Women's T20 World Cup semi-finals: Scenarios explained Women's T20 World Cup: Harmanpreet Kaur's India have it all to play for against Australia in their final Group A clash, with a place in the semi-finals still up for grabs and the qualification race set to go down to the final day of the group stage.

The Jai Moondra story: How a Master's degree scripted dream Ireland debut vs India IND vs IRE: Jai Moondra moved to Ireland in 2021 to pursue a Master's degree in Electronics and Communication. Five years later, the Indian-born cricketer realised his dream of playing international cricket by representing his adopted nation.

Lionel Messi to be benched for Argentina's final group-stage game vs Jordan Lionel Messi will start on the bench against Jordan as Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni rotates his squad after sealing top spot in Group J. Messi is expected to feature later in the World Cup clash.

Reece James out, Saka fit: Tuchel reveals England team news before Panama clash England defender Reece James has been ruled out of the crucial World Cup Group L clash against Panama with a hamstring injury. Thomas Tuchel confirmed Bukayo Saka, Declan Rice and Elliot Anderson are fit.

URU 0-1 ESP HIGHLIGHTS: Spain, Cabo Verde into knockouts, Uruguay crash outUruguay (URU) vs Spain (ESP) Live, FIFA World Cup 2026: (URU 0-1 ESP) Group H has exploded into life at the break. Spain have one foot in the knockouts after capitalising on a costly Muslera error, while Cape Verde are just 45 minutes away from a historic last-32 berth. As things stand, Uruguay are heading for a stunning World Cup exit.

Dembele steals the Mbappe vs Haaland show as France breeze past NorwayFIFA World Cup 2026: France marched into the Round of 32 with a perfect Group I record after Ousmane Dembele's stunning first-half hat-trick inspired a 4-1 win over Norway. Elsewhere, Senegal crushed Iraq 5-0 to keep their hopes alive of progressing as one of the World Cup's best third-placed teams.

Senegal's historic five-goal blitz breathes life into World Cup campaignSenegal vs Iraq, FIFA World Cup 2026: Senegal thrashed 10-man Iraq 5-0 to keep their FIFA World Cup knockout hopes alive, becoming the first African nation to score five goals in a men's World Cup match.

Ousmane Dembele silences doubters with historic World Cup hat-trick for FranceOusmane Dembele's historic World Cup hat-trick against Norway marked the defining moment of his France career, capping years of perseverance with a record-breaking performance.

India hammer Pakistan 7-1 in FIH Pro League, extend decade-long dominanceIndia produced a commanding comeback to thrash Pakistan 7-1 in the FIH Pro League after conceding an early penalty-corner goal, with Harmanpreet Singh leading a clinical attacking display. The emphatic victory puts India seventh on the points table, above Spain and Pakistan.

Jemimah tells India to go on the attack against AustraliaWomen's T20 World Cup, INDW vs AUSW: Jemimah Rodrigues said that India need to be aggressive and can't afford to be defensive when they face Australia at the Lord's Cricket Ground on Sunday.

FIFA World Cup Highlights: France crush Norway, Senagal alive after 5-0 Iraq winNorway vs France Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: France marched into the knockouts with a commanding 4-1 win over Norway, finishing Group I with a perfect record. Elsewhere, Senegal kept their World Cup dream alive by thrashing Iraq 5-0 to boost their hopes of sneaking through as one of the eight best third-placed teams.

Sri Lanka vs Scotland: Highlights from ManchesterSL-W vs SCO-W, Women's T20 World Cup Highlights: Sri Lanka have won the toss and opted to bowl first at Old Trafford in Manchester. Chamari Athapaththu's troops are looking to stay alive in the competition.

After loss in captaincy debut, Shreyas Iyer fires a warning to Team IndiaIRE vs IND: Ireland beat India by 34 runs in Belfast, their first international win over them. Shreyas Iyer warned his side against complacency as the series now hangs on Sunday's final T20I.

Ireland ruin Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut to seal historic T20I winIRE vs IND, 1st T20I: Lorcan Tucker's Ireland stunned Shreyas Iyer's India on Friday, beating the reigning T20 world champions by 34 runs in Belfast. Chasing 183 for victory, India were bowled out for 148, as Ireland registered their first-ever win over India in international cricket.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi omission vs Ireland sparks social media frenzyShreyas Iyer confirmed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi will miss India's first T20I against Ireland in Belfast. The call has sharpened the debate between rewarding form and backing an established core.

Who is Rounak Chouhan? 18-year-old from Chhattisgarh stuns Chou Tien Chen at US OpenUS Open 2026: 18-year-old Rounak Chouhan upset top seed Chou Tien Chen in straight games at the US Open. The win marked his first top-10 scalp and underlined his rise from India's junior ranks.

Alexander Isak scolds Anthony Elanga over knockout qualification frustrationFIFA World Cup 2026: Anthony Elanga scored in Sweden's 1-1 draw with Japan before thinking the result had knocked them out. The mix-up quickly gave way to relief as Sweden advanced among the best third-placed teams.

Why India delayed Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's debut in IrelandIndia left 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi out of the first T20I against Ireland in Belfast. Shreyas Iyer said senior World Cup winners were backed, a call Sanjay Manjrekar hailed as merit-based.

Who will Serena Williams face on her Slam comeback? Wimbledon draw explainedWimbledon 2026: Serena Williams will begin her campaign against Australia's rising star Maya Joint after receiving a wildcard for her first Grand Slam singles appearance since the 2022 US Open.

Wimbledon draw: Novak Djokovic in Sinner's half, faces tough road to quartersWimbledon 2026: Novak Djokovic has landed in Jannik Sinner's half of the draw. Carlos Alcaraz's withdrawal opens the field, but a demanding route still shapes Djokovic's bid for a 25th Grand Slam title.

1st T20I Highlights: Iyer's captaincy debut ends in vain as Ireland scripts historyIndia (IND) vs Ireland (IRE) Highlights, 1st T20I: IND (148/10 in 18.5 ov), chasing 183. Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut ended in vain as Ireland scripted history by winning their first ever T20I against India.

Ecuador coach's euphoric celebration after mega-upset vs Germany goes viralEcuador coach Sebastian Beccacece's emotional celebration after his side's dramatic 2-1 win over Germany went viral as the South American nation reached the FIFA World Cup knockout stage for the first time since 2006. Beccacece sprinted into the stands to embrace his wife and supporters after Ecuador completed a remarkable turnaround.

Germany camp divided? Undav, Kimmich contradict Nagelsmann after Ecuador defeatGermany manager Julian Nagelsmann dismissed suggestions of a lack of desire as "nonsense", but captain Joshua Kimmich and striker Deniz Undav painted a very different picture after their side's 2-1 defeat to Ecuador in their final Group E match at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
